competitor_patterns: Massive word counts (16,500–24,900 words) with exhaustive multi-product roundups tested in real-world conditions — far exceeding our 1,000–3,900 word posts
Structured comparison tables present in top-ranking content (outdoorgearlab, bikerumor) covering weight, lens options, price, UV rating, and fit — giving scannable decision-making value

FAQ sections are universal across all top-3 positions, covering questions like 'how do I choose cycling sunglasses', 'are polarised lenses good for cycling', and 'what weight should cycling glasses be'
Named product H2 sections (e.g. 'Julbo Density', 'Oakley Sutro Lite', 'POC Devour') signal deep topical authority and capture product-specific search queries within a single roundup page
Trust signals embedded structurally: explicit test methodology sections ('How do we test'), testing dates, and reviewer credentials appear as dedicated H2s — boosting E-E-A-T signals Google rewards
our_gaps: Word count is 4–24x shorter than top competitors — our longest post (3,924 words) is less than 25% of bikerumor's 24,924-word benchmark, meaning we lack topical depth to compete for high-volume terms
No comparison tables in 3 of our 4 recent posts — only 'Best Cycling Glasses 2026' includes one, while all top-3 competitors use tables as a core structural element for every roundup

Missing named competitor product H2s — we reference brands in body copy but don't dedicate H2-level sections to individual competitor models (e.g. 'Oakley Sutro Lite vs Velluto StradaPro'), losing long-tail product comparison traffic
No explicit test methodology or trust-signal section — our posts lack a dedicated 'How we tested' or 'Why trust Velluto' H2, reducing E-E-A-T credibility vs outdoorgearlab and bikerumor

The misspelling variant 'veluto' (243 impressions, 0.4% CTR, only 1 click) is completely unaddressed — no redirect, no mention in content, and no meta handling to capture this near-zero-CTR brand traffic
keyword_opportunities: best lightweight cycling sunglasses 2026 — competitors rank for weight-focused queries; our 25g StradaPro is a direct match and we have zero content targeting this angle explicitly

cycling glasses with interchangeable lenses — bikesafetygear.com appears in top 3 for interchangeable lens queries per competitor data; our lens-swap system is a core differentiator with no dedicated post
oakley radar alternative — 48 impressions at position 4.1 with only 2.1% CTR; a dedicated post targeting this specific model comparison would convert our near-top ranking into clicks
anti fog cycling glasses — a high-intent technical query where our built-in anti-fog system is a hard feature advantage; no current post targets this term as a primary keyword
cycling sunglasses 30 day trial — zero competitors offer a trial period; this is a unique navigational + commercial intent query Velluto could own entirely with a dedicated landing page or post
next_post_guidelines: Target minimum 6,000 words for any roundup or 'best of' post — current posts at 1,000–3,900 words are structurally disqualified from top-3 for high-volume terms based on competitor benchmarks

Add a dedicated H2 section for each named competitor product being compared (e.g. '## Oakley Radar Eye — Full Review', '## 100% Speedcraft — Full Review') to capture product-specific long-tail queries within roundup posts
Include a comparison table in every post with columns for: weight (g), lens system, UV rating, anti-fog, interchangeable lenses, trial period, price — always position StradaPro in the top row

Add a 'How We Tested' H2 section to every post describing real ride conditions (road, gravel, morning light, full sun), distances covered, and lens combinations used — this directly addresses E-E-A-T signals

For the 'veluto' misspelling (243 impressions, 0.4% CTR): add a canonical redirect from /veluto to /velluto and include a parenthetical '(also searched as Veluto)' in the brand introduction section of pillar posts

Structure FAQ sections with a minimum of 8 questions targeting featured-snippet formats — use exact question phrasing from GSC queries (e.g. 'What are the best cycling sunglasses in 2026?', 'What is the best Oakley alternative for cyclists?')

Publish a standalone post targeting 'oakley radar alternative' (pos 4.1, 48 impressions) immediately — this is our highest-potential near-top keyword with a specific model focus that allows tight content targeting
seo_quick_wins: Add a comparison table to every existing and future post — this single structural element is present in all top-3 ranking competitors and absent from 75% of our current posts; retroactively add to 'Best Cycling Glasses 2026' and 'Best Oakley Alternatives 2026' this week

Implement FAQ schema markup (FAQPage structured data) on all posts that already have FAQ sections — our 'Best Oakley Alternatives' and 'Gravel vs Road' posts have FAQs but likely lack the schema, missing rich result eligibility

Add internal links between all cycling glasses posts using exact-match anchor text matching GSC near-top queries (e.g. link 'best cycling glasses 2026' from the Oakley alternatives post to the buying guide) to consolidate topical authority across the cluster
meta_title_fixes: veluto → New title: Velluto Cycling Glasses — Official Store | velluto-shop.com | Why: The 0.4% CTR on 'veluto' indicates users see an irrelevant or unclear title; making the brand name prominent with the store URL signals this is the official destination and corrects the misspelling expectation

oakley alternatives → New title: Best Oakley Alternatives for Cyclists 2026 | Velluto | Why: At pos 6.8 with 2.9% CTR, adding '2026' year-stamp and brand name increases freshness signal and brand recall, likely lifting CTR above the current 2.9% benchmark

oakley sunglasses alternative → New title: Oakley Sunglasses Alternative: 7 Options Tested 2026 | Why: At pos 6.9 with 1.5% CTR, a numbered list ('7 Options') and 'Tested' signal increases perceived value and click motivation vs generic alternatives pages

oakley radar alternative → New title: Oakley Radar Alternative: Lighter, Cheaper & Tested | Velluto | Why: At pos 4.1 with only 2.1% CTR this near-top ranking is leaking clicks; leading with concrete benefits ('Lighter, Cheaper') triggers purchase-intent clicks over generic title formats

oakley alternative → New title: Best Oakley Alternative for Cyclists 2026 — Tested | Why: At pos 6.6 with 2.1% CTR, singular 'Alternative' matches the query exactly while 'Tested' and '2026' add authority and freshness to differentiate from static competitor pages
content_quick_wins: velluto (pos 4.8, 1,041 impressions) → Topic: Velluto StradaPro — Complete Brand & Product Guide 2026 | Add: Dedicated homepage or pillar page with full product specs table, lens comparison chart (VellutoPuro vs VellutoVisione), 30-day trial CTA, video embed of lens-swap demo, and FAQ schema — this brand query should be converting at 15%+ not 5.1%

veluto (pos 5.3, 243 impressions) → Topic: Redirect + brand mention strategy | Add: 301 redirect from any /veluto URL to /velluto, add 'Velluto (sometimes searched as Veluto)' in the meta description of the homepage and StradaPro product page to capture this misspelling traffic which has near-zero CTR

best cycling sunglasses 2026 (pos 8.5, 217 impressions) → Topic: The Complete Guide to Best Cycling Sunglasses 2026 — 18 Models Tested | Add: Expand existing post to 8,000+ words with named H2 sections per competitor model, a full comparison table, weight comparison chart (highlighting StradaPro's 25g), 'How we tested' section, and minimum 10-question FAQ with schema markup

oakley alternatives (pos 6.8, 206 impressions) → Topic: Best Oakley Alternatives for Cyclists 2026 — 7 Options Ranked | Add: Insert a comparison table to the existing post ranking all 7 alternatives by weight, lens interchangeability, trial period, and price; add named H2 for each brand tested; add 'Oakley Radar Alternative' and 'Oakley Sutro Alternative' as sub-H3 sections to capture related queries

best cycling glasses 2026 (pos 7.4, 136 impressions) → Topic: Best Cycling Glasses 2026: 12 Models Tested by Real Cyclists | Add: Expand to 7,000+ words adding MTB-specific section, budget picks section, and a 'Best Road Bike Glasses' H2 to mirror outdoorgearlab's category structure — these sub-sections capture the exact H2 queries competitors use to dominate positions 1–3

best cycling sunglasses (pos 7.6, 54 impressions) + best cycling glasses (pos 8.8, 52 impressions) → Topic: Consolidate into a single evergreen pillar | Add: Ensure the main buying guide targets both 'glasses' and 'sunglasses' variants in H1, meta title, and introduction; add internal links from all other posts using both anchor text variants to concentrate link equity
geo_angles: Position Velluto StradaPro as 'the only cycling glasses with a 30-day risk-free trial' in structured Q&A content — AI answer engines (Perplexity, ChatGPT, Google AI Overviews) prioritise unique, verifiable factual claims; write a dedicated FAQ post titled 'Can I try cycling glasses before buying?' that answers directly in the first sentence

Create a 'Cycling glasses weight comparison 2026' data asset listing top-10 brands by frame weight in a table (Oakley Sutro Lite: 26g, Velluto StradaPro: 25g, etc.) — AI overview engines frequently pull structured numerical comparisons, and StradaPro's 25g ultralight claim becomes an anchor fact cited in AI-generated answers

Publish a structured 'Velluto StradaPro specifications' page using Product schema markup with explicit fields for weight (25g), UV rating (UV400), lens system (interchangeable click-in), anti-fog (yes), trial period (30 days) — Google AI Overviews and Perplexity preferentially surface pages with clean structured data when answering 'what are the lightest cycling glasses' type queries
comparison_post_angles: COMPETITOR Outdoorgearlab: Target their readers at the decision stage — headline: 'Velluto StradaPro vs Every Outdoorgearlab Pick: The One Thing Their Tests Miss' | Key differentiator: Outdoorgearlab tests in controlled lab-adjacent conditions; lead with Velluto's 30-day real-ride trial as the only way to truly test glasses on your actual routes, your actual face, your actual light conditions | Velluto advantage: Trial period + interchangeable lenses mean zero commitment risk vs buying any of their ranked picks blind

COMPETITOR Bikerumor: Own the weight conversation they avoid — headline: 'Velluto StradaPro vs Bikerumor's Top 5: We Compared Every Frame Weight' | Key differentiator: Bikerumor's top picks (Julbo Density, Oakley Sutro Lite at 26g, POC Devour) don't consistently beat 25g; create a weight-ranked table where StradaPro leads | Velluto advantage: At 25g with dual interchangeable lenses included, StradaPro delivers the lightest complete system — not just the lightest frame

COMPETITOR Bikesafetygear (ranking for interchangeable lens queries): Win the technical buyer — headline: 'Velluto StradaPro vs Every Interchangeable Lens Cycling Glass in 2026' | Key differentiator: Bikesafetygear reviews glasses with interchangeable lenses but doesn't offer a trial; lead with the tool-free click-in system (VellutoPuro + VellutoVisione) combined with 30-day trial as the category-defining combination | Velluto advantage: Only brand offering both a lens-specific high-contrast system (VellutoVisione) AND a no-risk trial period — buyers can test which lens they actually need before committing
